channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Israel presses forward on two fronts and reports 8 combat deaths as fears of a wider war mount
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Israel pressed forward on two fronts Wednesday, pursuing a ground incursion into Lebanon against Hezbollah that left eight Israeli soldiers dead and conducting strikes in Gaza that killed dozens, including children.
item.dc:creator: KAREEM CHEHAYEB and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel defies Biden in Lebanon, testing US support
item.description: The Israeli operation in Lebanon is exposing the failures of U.S. efforts to de-escalate the Middle East conflict, raising the likelihood of a full-scale war against Hezbollah and testing President Biden's support for Israel's expanding military campaigns.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Dominican Republic will deport up to 10,000 Haitians a week, citing an 'excess' of immigrants
item.description: The Dominican Republic says it will start massive deportations of Haitians living illegally in the country and expel up to 10,000 of them a week.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Mexico's Sheinbaum keeps doing morning briefings, though her style is unlike her predecessor's
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Mexico's new President Claudia Sheinbaum started her day Wednesday much like her political mentor, ex-President Andrés Manuel López Obrador, began most of his: with an early morning news briefing from the National Palace.
item.dc:creator: FABIOLA SÁNCHEZ, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Catholic reform debate launched by Pope Francis leans away from ordained roles for women
item.description: ROME (AP) — Debate was leaning away from allowing women to take on ordained roles in the Catholic Church as the second phase of Pope Francis’ reform project opened Wednesday with an agenda topped by calls for women to take up more positions of responsibility Francis presided over an opening Mass in
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Chinese Communist Party marks 75 years of rule as economic, security threats persist
item.description: China is marking the 75th year of Communist Party rule. The state-controlled media reported on China's progress and stability, with no mention of the many challenges facing the nation.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US bans new types of goods from China over allegations of forced labor
item.description: The Department of Homeland Security has announced that it would ban the import of goods from a Chinese steel manufacturer and a Chinese maker of artificial sweetener.
item.dc:creator: Didi Tang, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: WATCH: US destroyer fires ballistic missile interceptors to defend Israel against Iranian barrage
item.description: The USS Cole and USS Bulkeley fired about a dozen ballistic missile interceptors to defend Israel from a barrage of nearly 200 Iranian missiles on Tuesday, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: CIA makes it easier for potential informants to share tips
item.description: The CIA is offering advice to people in North Korea, Iran and China who want to share information with intelligence officials — without getting caught.
item.dc:creator: David Klepper, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Putin signs law granting immunity to criminal defendants who join the army
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin signed a law Wednesday that will allow criminal defendants to skip prosecution and potential prison time if they enlist in the army.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Israel bans UN secretary-general over anti-Israel actions: 'Doesn't deserve to set foot on Israeli soil'
item.description: The United Nations has increasingly faced criticism over the past year for how officials have responded to and handled the conflicts in the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Police patrols heightened in Jewish communities ahead of holidays
item.description: Police patrols will be heightened in Jewish communities ahead of a set of important holidays in Judaism, police across the U.S. told The Hill on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: 4 Russian journalists accused of working for a Navalny group go on trial in Moscow
item.description: TALLINN, Estonia (AP) — Four Russian journalists went on trial in Moscow on Wednesday after being accused of working for an anti-corruption group founded by the late Russian opposition politician Alexei Navalny, which was designated by authorities as an extremist organization in 2021.
item.dc:creator: DASHA LITVINOVA, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: 8 Israeli soldiers killed in Lebanon as Netanyahu says IDF engaged in 'tough war' with Hezbollah
item.description: Israel's military announced Wednesday that eight soldiers have died during "combat in southern Lebanon" as troops are wiping out Hezbollah infrastructure.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Prince Harry visits the small mountain kingdom of Lesotho, where he's called 'the warrior'
item.description: MASERU, Lesotho (AP) — Prince Harry is visiting the small mountain kingdom of Lesotho having co-founded a youth charity there in 2006 in honor of his late mother, Princess Diana.
item.dc:creator: KEKETSO PHAKELA, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Ancient painting revealed in Egypt beneath layers of bird poop
item.description: During restoration of the Temple of Edfu, artwork was discovered beneath layers of bird poop, dust and soot covering the temple walls.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel's multilayered air-defense system passes another test in fending off Iranian missile strike
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israel's multilayered air-defense system appears to have passed another test after fending off Iran's latest missile barrage. In Tuesday's night's strike, Iran fired over 180 missiles toward Israel.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Australia's online dating industry adopts code of conduct to keep users safer
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — A code of conduct will be enforced on the online dating industry to better protect Australian users after research found that three-in-four people suffer some form of sexual violence through the platforms, Australia’s government said on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Netanyahu says Iran 'will pay' for missile attack
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said Iran “will pay” for the 180 missiles launched toward his country on Tuesday. “This evening, Iran made a big mistake — and it will pay for it,” the leader said during a security Cabinet meeting.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: US readiness plays 'significant' role in fending off Iranian attack on Israel
item.description: The U.S. once again assured Israel it stands ready to defend it against Iran just hours prior to Tehran launching a second large scale strike on Israel in which some 200 missiles were fired at it.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The five smallest countries in the world cover less than 50 square miles combined
item.description: Vatican City, Monaco, Nauru, Tuvalu and San Marino make up the world's smallest countries. The biggest of the group, San Marino, is only 25 square miles.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mysteries of universe revealed? Hardly. But CERN still fascinates, discovers on its 70th anniversary
item.description: GENEVA (AP) — The research center that is home to the world’s largest particle accelerator is celebrating its 70th anniversary on Tuesday, with the physicists who run it aiming to unlock secrets about dark matter and other mysteries to promote science for peace in today's conflict-darkened world.
item.dc:creator: JAMEY KEATEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Iran fires multiple missile strikes across Israel
item.description: Iran has launched a missile attack against Israel, the Israel Defense Force (IDF) said Tuesday, warning residents to shelter in place.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NATO's new chief makes Ukraine support a top priority and says he will work with any US president
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — The new head of NATO vowed on Tuesday to help shore up Western support for war-ravaged Ukraine and expressed confidence that he can work with whoever is elected president of the United States, the alliance's most powerful member, in November.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Israeli ministers frustrated over US, IDF leak on Lebanon operation: Report
item.description: Israeli ministers agree to operational strategy for ground invasion hours after officials in the US confirmed a 'limited' invasion of Lebanon was coming, drawing frustration from Jerusalem.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Video shows Russian fighter jet flying within feet of US F-16 near Alaska
item.description: The release of the video comes after a series of Russian incursions into the Alaska Air Defense Identification Zone.
item.dc:creator: MARK THIESSEN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Russian fighter jet intercepted near Alaska by NORAD forces after ‘unsafe, unprofessional’ interaction
item.description: North American Aerospace Defense Command (NORAD) released a video showing the dangerous moment a Russian Su-35 fighter pilot flew right in front of a NORAD F-16 fighter pilot over the Alaska Air Defense Zone.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Flooding, landslides kill nearly 200 in Nepal
item.description: At least 193 people have been confirmed dead following severe flooding and landslides in Nepal, where heavy weekend rainfall was reported.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israeli raids against Hezbollah signal a wider ground incursion into Lebanon could come soon
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — The Israeli military launched small ground raids against Hezbollah and sealed off communities along its northern border on Monday as Israeli artillery pounded southern Lebanon and signals grew that more forces could soon be sent across the border to fight the Iran-backed militants.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, SAMY MAGDY, ABBY SEWELL and NATALIE MELZER Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Hezbollah's acting leader vows to fight on after Nasrallah's death
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Hezbollah's acting leader vowed Monday to continue battling Israel and said the militant group was prepared for a long fight even after much of its top command was wiped out, including its leader, Hassan Nasrallah.
item.dc:creator: ABBY SEWELL and NATALIE MELZER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: US airstrikes on Syria kill 37 militants affiliated with extremist groups
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Two U.S. airstrikes in Syria killed 37 militants affiliated with the Islamic State group and an al-Qaeda-linked group, the U.S. military said Sunday. It said two of the dead were senior militants. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Reward offered for Iranian in alleged Bolton assassination plot
item.description: Iranian Vice-President Mohammad Javad Zarif rejected accusations that Iran has tried to assassinate government critics overseas.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Netanyahu vows to use 'full force' against Hezbollah and dims hopes for a cease-fire
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu on Thursday vowed to carry out “full force” strikes against Hezbollah until it ceases firing rockets across the border, dimming hopes for a cease-fire proposal put forth by U.S. and European officials.
item.dc:creator: TIA GOLDENBERG, BASSEM MROUE and MELANIE LIDMAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Harris blasts proposals for Ukraine to cede territory to Russia during Zelenskyy meeting
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — With American support for Ukraine at a partisan crossroads, Vice President Kamala Harris on Thursday slammed suggestions that Kyiv should cede territory for the sake of peace with Moscow as “dangerous and unacceptable.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, NewsNation
